note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves cell growth, lysis, and affinity chromatography to study protein interactions. The goal is to identify proteins that bind to specific phosphoinositides (PIs) conjugated to agarose beads.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A scientist in a lab setting prepares samples by adding PBS-G (PBS pH 7.4 supplemented with 6 mM glucose) to ice-cold tubes containing PIs conjugated to agarose beads.",
    "The scientist uses a pipette to add a solution to the tubes, ensuring precise measurements.",
    "Samples are incubated at room temperature for 1 hour.",
    "After incubation, the samples are centrifuged to separate the beads from the supernatant.",
    "The supernatant is then subjected to affinity chromatography using PI(3,4,5)P3 agarose beads.",
    "Protein samples are loaded onto the beads and washed to remove unbound proteins.",
    "The bound proteins are eluted and analyzed using Western blotting."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "PBS-G solution",
    "Ice-cold tubes",
    "Agarose beads conjugated to various PIs",
    "Western blotting kit",
    "Microcentrifuge",
    "PI(3,4,5)P3 agarose beads"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Microcentrifuge",
    "Western blotting apparatus",
    "Pipette",
    "Incubator",
    "Centrifuge"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [

    "Time of incubation (1 hour)",
    "Volume of supernatant after centrifugation",
